questionText: Complete the sentence with the correct passive form: English ______ by millions of people around the world.
explanation: The passive voice is formed with be + past participle. Here, 'English' is the object of the action 'speak'. The present simple passive is 'is spoken' because English is singular.

questionText: Transform this active sentence into the passive voice: The chef prepares the meal every evening.
options: The meal is prepared by the chef every evening.
The meal was prepared by the chef every evening.
The meal prepares the chef every evening.
The meal is being prepared by the chef every evening.
explanation: Active: subject (chef) + verb (prepares) + object (meal). Passive: object becomes subject ('the meal'), verb 'to be' in the same tense (present simple → 'is') + past participle 'prepared', then 'by + agent'.

questionText: Choose the correct passive form in the past tense: The bridge ______ in 1995.
explanation: The action happened in 1995 (past simple). Passive: 'was' (singular subject 'bridge') + past participle 'built'.

questionText: Which sentence is correctly written in the passive voice?
options: The letter is write by John.
The letter was written by John.
The letter were written by John.
explanation: Correct passive structure: subject + appropriate form of 'to be' + past participle. 'Was written' is correct past simple passive. 'Were' would be used for plural subjects.

questionText: Identify the agent in this passive sentence: Hamlet was written by Shakespeare.
explanation: The agent is the person or thing performing the action. In passive voice, it is introduced by 'by'. Here, 'Shakespeare' is the agent. The option 'by Shakespeare' includes the preposition, but the question asks for the agent, so 'Shakespeare' is correct.

questionText: Put this sentence into the passive voice: Someone is repairing my phone.
explanation: The active sentence is present continuous. Passive: subject (my phone) + 'is being' (present continuous of 'to be') + past participle 'repaired'.

questionText: Complete with the correct passive form: The letters ______ yesterday.
explanation: 'Yesterday' indicates past simple. Subject 'letters' is plural → 'were' + past participle 'sent'.

questionText: Choose the correct future passive: The results ______ tomorrow.
explanation: Future simple passive: will + be + past participle. 'Will be announced' is correct. 'Are announced' would be present simple, not future.

questionText: Which option correctly shows the passive form with a modal verb?
explanation: With modals (must, can, should, etc.), passive = modal + be + past participle. 'Must be done' is correct.

questionText: Transform into passive: They have finished the project.
explanation: Active present perfect → passive present perfect: subject + have/has + been + past participle. 'The project has been finished' is correct.

questionText: Read the sentence and choose the correct passive: People speak French in this part of Cameroon.
options: French is spoken in this part of Cameroon.
French was spoken in this part of Cameroon.
French is being spoken in this part of Cameroon.
French speaks in this part of Cameroon.
explanation: General truth / habit → present simple passive. 'Is spoken' is correct. The agent 'people' is omitted because it is unimportant.

questionText: Why is the agent omitted in this sentence? The thief was arrested last night.
options: Because the agent is obvious or unimportant.
Because the sentence is in the past.
explanation: In passive voice, we omit 'by + agent' when the agent is unknown, obvious, or unimportant. Here, 'by the police' is understood, so it is omitted.

questionText: Which sentence emphasizes the receiver of the action correctly?
options: A speech was given by the president.
The president was giving a speech.
explanation: The passive voice 'A speech was given...' emphasizes the receiver ('a speech') rather than the doer ('the president'). This is useful when the receiver is more important.

questionText: Choose the correct passive: They will not admit children under 10.
options: Children under 10 will not be admitted.
explanation: Future simple negative passive: will not + be + past participle. 'Will not be admitted' is correct.

questionText: Which is the correct passive form of: Someone stole my wallet yesterday.
options: My wallet has been stolen yesterday.
explanation: Past simple active → past simple passive. 'My wallet' (singular) + 'was' + past participle 'stolen'. 'Yesterday' confirms past simple, not present perfect.
